def modify_features(df_input) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """
    Thought 1: The absolute difference between Left-Weight and Right-Weight can capture the imbalance in weight distribution.
    New Feature 1: weight_difference | weight_difference = abs(Left-Weight - Right-Weight)
    """
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    """
    This function generates new features that capture meaningful patterns and relationships in the balance scale data, 
    and refines the existing feature set by removing redundant or less informative features.
    
    Thought 2: New features such as the absolute difference between Left-Distance and Right-Distance, 
    the ratio of Left-Distance to Right-Distance, the difference between Left-Weight and Right-Distance, 
    the ratio of Left-Weight to Right-Distance, and the product of weight difference and distance difference 
    can provide more insights and correlations in the data.
    Thought 3: The minimum, maximum, and average values of each feature across different distances can 
    be used to derive new features that capture the patterns in the data.
    Thought 4: The residual of the weight difference from the mean weight difference can be used to 
    capture the variability in the weight difference across different distances.
    Thought 5: The rank of weight difference and distance difference can be used to derive new features.
    Thought 6: The standard deviation of weight and distance across different distances can be used 
    to capture the variability in the data.
    Thought 7: The difference between weight and distance across different distances can be used 
    to capture the correlation between weight and distance.
    Thought 8: The product of weight and distance across different distances can be used to 
    capture the interaction between weight and distance.
    Thought 9: The ratio of weight to distance can be used to capture the balance between weight and distance.
    Thought 10: The standard score (z-score) of weight and distance across different distances can be used 
    to capture the variability and outliers in the data.
    Thought 11: The sigmoid of weight and distance across different distances can be used to capture 
    the non-linear relationships in the data.
    Thought 12: The logarithm of weight and distance across different distances can be used to capture 
    the non-linear relationships and outliers in the data.
    """
    
    df_output = df_input.copy()
    
    # Calculate new features
    df_output['distance_diff'] = abs(df_output['Left-Distance'] - df_output['Right-Distance'])
    df_output['weight_ratio'] = df_output['Left-Weight'] / df_output['Right-Weight']
